goal: Close the production vector engine with explicit capability, topology, fallback and release contracts.

included: candidate engine availability and deterministic fallback
final artifact and corpus release gates
excluded: image classification and automatic mode confidence; tracked by the AI analyzer roadmap
SaaS billing, authentication and user-interface work

phases.title: Finite capability and closure contract
acceptance_criteria: A finite, machine-readable four-phase roadmap is committed and schema-validated.
Every production mode has a non-empty candidate plan and at least one non-optional candidate.
Every configured candidate references a known engine and every current limitation has exactly one closure phase.
Completed-phase evidence paths exist and production behavior remains unchanged.

phases.title: Deterministic centerline fallback closure
acceptance_criteria: The OpenCV fallback traces a deterministic skeleton graph into open centerline paths instead of contouring the skeleton outline.
Short spurs are pruned by the configured minimum branch length while endpoints and multi-way junctions remain connected.
The candidate report identifies the fallback backend and exposes measurable topology/confidence data.
Fallback output cannot be marked production_ready when topology measurement is unavailable or violates the centerline contract.
Line, polyline, T-junction, cross-junction, loop, spur-pruning and repeat-digest regression fixtures pass.

phases.title: Curve-preserving cutout and topology closure
acceptance_criteria: The production API never polygon-flattens Bezier or arc geometry for shape_stacking=cutouts.
A promotion-ready canonical non-overlapping face document or a curve-preserving counter representation is used; otherwise the request fails closed to stacked output.
No partial topology mutation is published and transform, digest and face/path coverage invariants are rechecked before export.
Visual regression satisfies seam_ratio <= 0.002 and halo_ratio <= 0.02 without unbounded command inflation.
Curved counters, nested holes, adjacent colors, unsupported transforms and dependency-unavailable regressions pass.

phases.title: All-mode artifact and corpus release closure
acceptance_criteria: Every explicit production mode has a representative deterministic three-run corpus result or an explicit fail-closed unavailable/needs_review verdict.
Final artifacts contain no bitmap payload, non-finite coordinate, open required cycle, stale score or mismatched output digest.
Artifact gates hold: ink_recall >= 0.995, ink_precision >= 0.975, component_delta == 0, seam_ratio <= 0.002 and halo_ratio <= 0.02 for the in-domain release corpus.
Photo/continuous-tone cases remain an explicit needs_review product limit rather than a false production_ready result.
Exact final artifact, hard SVG regression, all-mode corpus and benchmark release workflows are mandatory and green.
